project_name: mysql to bigquery data ingestion pipeline

columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier; primary key and first component of the upsert merge key used by sp_mysqltobq_load.sql

columns.name: first_name
columns.description: Employee first name; sourced from MySQL FIRST_NAME varchar(50)

columns.name: last_name
columns.description: Employee last name; sourced from MySQL LAST_NAME varchar(50)

columns.name: email
columns.description: Employee email address; sourced from MySQL EMAIL varchar(100)

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Employee phone number; nullable; sourced from MySQL PHONE_NUMBER varchar(20)

columns.name: hire_date
columns.description: Date the employee was hired; sourced from MySQL HIRE_DATE date

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role code; sourced from MySQL JOB_ID varchar(20); second clustering column

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee salary with two decimal places; sourced from MySQL SALARY decimal(8,2)

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage with two decimal places; nullable; sourced from MySQL COMMISSION_PCT decimal(4,2)

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: Self-referencing FK to employee_id identifying the employee's direct manager; NULL for top-level managers; sourced from MySQL MANAGER_ID decimal(10,0)

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier; nullable; sourced from MySQL DEPARTMENT_ID decimal(4,0); first clustering column

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employee status (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE); sourced from MySQL STATUS varchar(10); third clustering column

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Record creation timestamp; sourced from MySQL CREATED_DATE timestamp; assumed UTC

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Record last-updated timestamp; second component of the upsert merge key; DAY-level partition column driving incremental change-tracking; NULL rows land in the BigQuery __NULL__ partition; sourced from MySQL UPDATED_DATE timestamp; assumed UTC

cardinality.notes: Many employees may share the same manager; manager_id is a self-referencing FK pointing to employee_id within stg_employees. Employees with no manager (top-level) carry a NULL manager_id.
